title: Kokoona ochracea
taxonomy: Kokoona ochracea is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It is native to Madagascar and is considered an important part of the region's biodiversity. The classification of Kokoona ochracea is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Magnoliids, order Magnoliales, family Annonaceae, genus Kokoona, and species K. ochracea. The plant is part of the larger Kokoona genus, which contains several other species native to Madagascar. Further research is needed to fully understand the classification and evolutionary history of Kokoona ochracea.

intro: Kokoona ochracea, also known as Yellow Kokoona or Yellow Kokoona tree, is a small deciduous tree or shrub native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits are attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Kokoona ochracea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties and health benefits. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in topical creams and ointments to treat skin conditions and wounds.
